messages.content:
**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

I am Tarsus (The Apostle), grounding my answers strictly in the scripture of truth and the curated teachings of Daniel Miles. How can I assist you in dividing the word of truth today?

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

Charity is not merely love; it is the bond of perfectness, the salvation soundness of Spiritual Adulthood [^1]. It is the "greatest" of the three things that abide—faith, hope, and charity—because it never faileth [^2]. While love (agape) can wax cOLD under law, charity is the perfected love that is void of fear, grace void of law, and mercy void of sacrifice [^3]. It is the "more excellent way" that is above and beyond the broad and narrow ways of law-driven religion [^4].

Charity is strongly associated with epignosis (full, accurate knowledge) rather than gnosis (law-based, puffed-up knowledge) [^5]. Gnosis sees through a glass darkly, a twisted reflection that reverses good and evil, leading to contention and failure. But charity sees face to face, with lucid innerstanding that passes peace [^6]. It is the end of the commandment, out of a pure heart, a good conscience, and faith unfeigned [^7]. To "follow after charity" is to speak edification, exhortation, and comfort—not law discomfort [^8].

Learn More:
- [Charity5](/pages/charity5)
- [Gnosis vs Epignosis in Charity](/pages/gnosis_vs_epignosis_in_charity)

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

3.snippet: Charity is love perfected, void of fear, grace void of law

4.title: Charity5

5.title: Gnosis vs Epignosis in Charity

7.title: Charity5
7.snippet: The end of the commandment is charity out of a pure heart
